blocks.content: Saturday morning. The kind of quiet that meant the week had actually ended and whatever came next was still far enough away to breathe.

I lay there for a minute thinking about Daniel's quiet. The different kind. The kind Jonathan had named. Daniel hadn't gone away — he'd gone somewhere I couldn't see. That was the part I needed to stay ready for.

I thought about what Mr. Cole said about Sunday. I'd told my mom on Thursday. She didn't call the school. Not yet. But I could see her deciding. Whatever Daniel did next was going to determine how long that lasted.
I thought about the notebook on the desk. One line in it. The rest blank.
I thought about Stacy DMing Jonathan. About the way he'd looked at the bleachers when she was writing. He'd told me not to be distracted and then sat at exactly the same crossroads I had.
Something about that made me feel better. Not because he was also in it — because it meant neither of us had it perfectly figured out. We were both trying.
I got up. Made breakfast. Looked out the window at the block going about its Saturday.

Whatever was coming next — Daniel's quiet, Crystal's number in my phone, Stacy in the bleachers, the routes that finally clicked — it was all still arriving. Still becoming whatever it was going to be.
I didn't know what Episode 4 looked like yet. But I'd learned something this episode that I was keeping: the good decision and the scary decision are usually the same one.
